texto: [T]he new weird represents a productive experiment in fantasy fiction. The New Wave of the 1960s and 1970s arguably embodied science fiction's claim to literary 'seriousness.' This desire for seriousness is not snobbery, as sometimes suggested by folks who overemphasize the entertainment function of speculative fiction; it's about recognition of the vast possibilities within the field.
explicacao: Citação de Darja Malcolm-Clarke sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “[T]he new weird represents a productive experiment in fantasy fiction. The New Wave of the 1960s …” articula tempo, fantasy, literary-criticism em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como literary-criticism e new-wave.
